title: Alchemilla vestita

intro: Alchemilla vestita, also known as Lady's Mantle, is a perennial herbaceous plant belonging to the Rosaceae family, characterized by its rounded, scalloped leaves and small, greenish-yellow flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and astringent properties, which have been found to aid in wound healing, reduce menstrual cramps, and alleviate digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Alchemilla vestita include flavonoids, tannins, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Alchemilla vestita include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to treat menstrual disorders, gastrointestinal issues, and skin problems.
